Combat Training
Street-Effective
Self Defence Plan

Muay Thai forms the base — it is the most battle-tested striking art for real confrontations. Supplemented with wrestling basics and BJJ survival. This combination makes you genuinely dangerous on your feet and non-helpless on the ground.

🥊 Muay Thai — Primary
The most complete striking art. 8 weapons: fists, elbows, knees, shins. Clinch control. Devastating in stand-up. Train this 2x/week minimum. YouTube: Lawrence Kenshin breakdowns for technique. Look for a local class — even once/week transforms your striking.
Foundation
🤼 Wrestling — Takedown Defence
Most street fights go to the ground. You need to know how to: sprawl (stop a takedown), clinch safely, and create space. Even 3 months of wrestling basics changes everything. Makes you unshakeable in close range.
Survival Layer
🥋 BJJ — Ground Survival
If you end up on the ground, BJJ keeps you safe and in control. Focus on: guard retention, getting back to your feet, basic choke defense. You don't need to become a grappler — just need enough to not panic on the ground.
Ground Safety
📅 Self Defence Day — Saturday Addon
Add to your Saturday routine: 20 mins shadow boxing (Muay Thai combos), 10 mins footwork drills, 10 mins sprawl practice. Watch one technique video per week and drill it 100 times. Consistency over intensity.
Weekly Practice
⚡ Calisthenics Strength
Real functional strength: push-up variations → archer push-ups → one-arm progressions. Pike push-ups → handstand wall holds → freestanding handstand. L-sit → tuck planche. Add 10 mins to every Friday workout. This builds the body control fighters have.
Athletic Power
🏃 Agility & Conditioning
Jump rope 10 mins daily — builds footwork, cardio, timing. The single best combat conditioning tool. 3x50m sprints twice/week builds explosive speed. A strong body with bad cardio loses every fight after 30 seconds.
Endurance Base

Facial Development
The Science of
Becoming Your Best Self

Your face is not fixed at 16. Bone remodeling continues until 21–23. The habits you build right now — posture, breathing, chewing, hydration, body fat — are actively shaping your facial structure. This is your window.

⚡ The Single Biggest Facial Upgrade
Lose body fat. A lean face at 12% body fat looks completely different from the same face at 22%. Jawline emerges. Cheekbones become visible. Hollow under-eyes reduce. This one change does more than every facial exercise combined — and it comes from your training and diet, not from any special technique.
👅
Tongue Posture — Mewing
At 16, your palate is still expanding. Correct tongue posture applies gentle upward pressure that supports maxillary development. This is backed by orthodontic literature.
1Press entire tongue flat to roof of mouth
2Lips sealed, teeth lightly together
3Breathe only through nose
4Hold this ALL day — it becomes automatic
5Especially while studying, walking, resting
👃
Nasal Breathing
Mouth breathing causes forward face growth, poor jaw development, and facial drooping over years. Nasal breathing filters air, improves oxygen uptake 20%, and maintains proper facial structure.
1Lips closed at all times except speaking/eating
2If mouth breathing at night: try mouth tape
3During exercise: push yourself to nasal breathe
4Takes 2–4 weeks to retrain the habit fully
🦷
Chewing Hard Foods
Jaw muscle hypertrophy (masseter growth) gives definition and width to the lower face. Hard chewing also promotes bone density in the jaw. Simple but genuinely effective over years.
1Eat raw carrots, tough meats, hard fruits daily
2Chew equally on BOTH sides
3Chew each mouthful 20–30 times
4Avoid only soft foods — they atrophy jaw muscles
🧍
Posture — The Hidden Facial Tool
Forward head posture (chin jutting forward) makes your face look worse from every angle. Perfect posture adds perceived height, sharpens the jawline view, and changes how your eyes appear from the front.
1Ears directly above shoulders
2Slight chin tuck — not forced, just neutral
3Chest open, shoulders back and DOWN
4Daily chin tucks: 3×15 reps every morning
5Wall angels: 3×12 daily to reset upper back
💧
Hydration & Sodium
Facial puffiness is often just water retention from high sodium and dehydration — your face looks bloated and undefined. Drop sodium and drink more water and your face literally looks different within 48 hours.
13–4 litres of water daily — non-negotiable
2Cut packaged snacks (extremely high sodium)
3Reduce added salt in food
4Morning: drink 500ml water before anything else
😴
Sleep Position & Quality
Long-term side sleeping creates facial asymmetry — the compressed side gets flatter over years. Sleep quality affects cortisol, which affects fat storage in the face. 9 hours before 11 PM is your formula.
1Sleep on your BACK when possible
2No phone 45 mins before bed
3Sleep before 11 PM every night
47.5–9 hours minimum
5Growth hormone peaks in first 90 mins of sleep
🏋️
Neck Training
A thick neck makes your jawline look more defined from the front. It also improves posture dramatically and is one of the most visible signs of athletic development that people notice subconsciously.
1Chin tuck isometrics: 3×10 reps, 10 sec hold
2Neck side isometrics: palm on temple, resist
3At gym: neck machine all 4 directions
4Deadlifts naturally build neck thickness too
🚫
What Does NOT Work
Save your time and money. These are myths or have zero evidence for physical facial change in healthy people.
✗Jaw exerciser devices — zero jawline change in studies
✗Face rollers for bone change — they reduce puffiness only
✗Mewing after 21–23 — bones fused, minimal effect
✗Spot fat reduction on face — doesn't exist
✗Gua sha for facial structure — cosmetic only
The face you have at 20
is the face you build at 16.
What to Expect
Year by Year
Year 1 — Foundation Fat loss visible, posture improving
Year 2 — Definition Muscle emerging, jaw more visible
Year 3 — Sculpted Real body recomposition complete
Year 4 — Final Form The version of Eno at 20
